seoTitle: How to Share a Power BI Report: Options Explained
metaDescription: Compare the ways to share a Power BI report, from sharing within your organization to publishing to the web, and pick the right one for you.
intro: Power BI offers several ways to share a report, and picking the right one depends on who needs access and how they should view it. This guide walks through the main sharing options, including sharing within your organization, publishing to the web, and exporting. It's meant for anyone who needs to get insights in front of stakeholders without confusion over which method to use.

steps.heading: Review your sharing options
steps.body: Before sharing, understand the main choices available, since organizational sharing, web publishing, and exporting each serve different purposes.

steps.heading: Share within your organization
steps.body: Use Power BI's internal sharing options to give colleagues access to a report while keeping it inside your organization's environment.

steps.heading: Publish to the web when appropriate
steps.body: For reports meant for a broader public audience, use the publish-to-web option, keeping in mind this makes the report publicly accessible.

steps.heading: Export reports when needed
steps.body: Export a report to a file format when someone needs an offline copy or a version outside the interactive Power BI environment.

steps.heading: Choose the right method for your audience
steps.body: Match the sharing method to your audience, whether that's internal teams, external clients, or the general public, so access stays appropriate.

faq.question: Is publishing to the web safe for sensitive reports?
faq.answer: No, publish to web makes a report publicly accessible on the internet, so it should be reserved for non-sensitive data only.

faq.question: Can I share a Power BI report with people outside my organization?
faq.answer: Yes, depending on your license and settings, you can share with external users, though options vary from internal sharing.

faq.question: What's the difference between sharing a report and exporting it?
faq.answer: Sharing gives access to the live, interactive report, while exporting creates a static file version, such as a PDF or PowerPoint.

faq.question: Do recipients need a Power BI license to view a shared report?
faq.answer: In many cases recipients need at least a free Power BI account or appropriate license, depending on how the report is shared.

faq.question: Can I control what parts of a report someone sees when I share it?
faq.answer: Yes, permissions and row-level security settings can be used to control what data and pages different viewers can access.
